title: Building an X-Band AESA T/R Channel Around a Single GaN Die: A Look at VSI's VPF FEM Family
gist: Viettel Semiconductor (Vietnam) details its VPF front-end module family integrating PA, LNA, and T/R switch on a single GaN-on-SiC 250nm die (via WIN Semiconductors) for X-band AESA radar, cutting board area ~60-70% per T/R channel and targeting seeker, fire-control, and UAV radar applications.

insights.claim: VSI's VPF family integrates PA, LNA, and a reflective SPDT T/R switch on a single GaN-on-SiC 250nm die (fabbed via WIN Semiconductors) in a QFN 7x5 mm package, eliminating inter-chip interconnects and board-level matching networks.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A merchant single-die X-band FEM from a Vietnamese fabless firm signals broadening non-Western defense-RF supply capability.
insights.quote: combining PA, LNA, and T/R switch on a single GaN die

insights.claim: At X-band, element spacing runs about 15 mm at 10 GHz, forcing each T/R channel's front-end to fit an antenna-geometry-defined footprint, which is why the 7x5 mm QFN outline was chosen with margin for the beamformer IC.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Physical element-spacing constraints, not assembly convenience, drive integration density; a hard design boundary for radar RF suppliers.
insights.quote: the front-end electronics for each T/R channel must fit within a footprint defined by the antenna array geometry, not by what is convenient to assemble
insights.locator: One of the persistent cost and size challenges

insights.claim: Implementing the T/R switch in GaN on the same process as the PA and LNA eliminates one chip-to-chip interface in each of the transmit and receive paths, reducing insertion loss and package parasitics versus separate PIN diode or GaAs switch dies.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Monolithic GaN switching is the technical differentiator reducing loss and part count against discrete GaAs/PIN implementations.
insights.quote: GaN SPDT switches on the same process as the PA and LNA eliminate one chip-to-chip interface in the transmit path and one in the receive path
insights.locator: The choice to implement the T/R switch in GaN

insights.claim: The integrated FEM cuts board area roughly 60-70% versus a discrete PA+LNA+switch implementation, eliminates three chip-to-chip interconnects, and reduces matching-network elements and failure points.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: For a 256-element array the area/assembly savings compound, a quantified cost and reliability argument for integrated FEMs.
insights.quote: the integrated FEM approach reduces board area by approximately 60–70%

insights.claim: The VPF1005 delivers >37 dBm (5W) saturated output via a two-stage PA, while the VPF1010 delivers >40 dBm (10W) via a three-stage PA, both maintaining PAE above 30% at 28V drain across the 8-11 GHz band.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: The 3 dB power step between parts maps to distinct radar tiers; PAE >30% is critical for array-level thermal budgets.
insights.quote: a two-stage design for VPF1005 and a three-stage design for VPF1010 to reach the additional 3 dB output power while maintaining PAE above 30%
insights.locator: Architecture: what goes on the die

insights.claim: Both parts achieve LNA noise figure below 3 dB with >20 dB gain across 8-11 GHz, and a 1 dB improvement in LNA NF translates directly to 1 dB improvement in radar detection range at constant transmit power.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: The direct NF-to-detection-range link makes receive-path noise the key performance lever buyers should benchmark.
insights.quote: a 1 dB improvement in LNA NF translates directly to 1 dB improvement in radar detection range at constant transmit power
insights.locator: Receive path: noise figure and LNA-switch isolation

insights.claim: The VPF design achieves greater than 25 dB TX-to-RX isolation in switch-off state, sufficient to prevent transmit power from driving the LNA into compression or damaging it during pulsed X-band radar duty cycles.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Isolation is the make-or-break integration spec for single-die FEMs; 25 dB defines the applications VSI can serve.
insights.quote: The VPF design achieves greater than 25 dB TX-to-RX isolation in switch-off state
insights.locator: Receive path: noise figure and LNA-switch isolation

insights.claim: Gain from input to PA output exceeds 25 dB, allowing direct drive from beamformer IC outputs without an intermediate driver stage in most phased-array architectures.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Eliminating a driver stage simplifies the T/R channel BOM and layout, reinforcing the integration cost case.
insights.quote: allowing direct drive from beamformer IC outputs without an intermediate driver stage

insights.claim: The FEM architecture with integrated T/R switch suits pulsed radar, whereas CW applications like LEO Satcom user terminals generally require separate PA and LNA with a diplexer, so the VPF series is positioned specifically for pulsed radar.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: The pulsed-vs-CW distinction defines VPF's addressable market and where it is deliberately not competing.
insights.quote: which is why the VPF series is positioned specifically for pulsed radar
insights.locator: The choice to implement the T/R switch in GaN

insights.claim: VPF1005's 37 dBm per element fits seeker radar arrays of 256-512 elements for 10-20 km detection, while VPF1010's 40 dBm targets 1,000+ element airborne fire-control arrays with hundreds-of-km standoff detection.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Clear per-element power-to-application mapping helps assess which defense radar programs each part addresses.

insights.claim: Both parts are silicon-proven, production-qualified IP available through Design & Reuse with GDSII on WIN Semiconductors 250nm GaN-on-SiC, and interface directly with VSI's VBF1044 X-band 4T4R CMOS beamformer IC.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A matched FEM-plus-beamformer IP offering lowers integration risk for array designers sourcing from Viettel.
insights.locator: Note on integration with VSI's beamformer IC
